description: Closed unit and quantity-kind registry. A non-blank string is not a unit: every stable numeric axis carries a code from `units`, and every quantity carries a `kind` from `quantityKinds` whose dimension must match that code's dimension. Dimensional compatibility is enforced by the semantic validator `unit.dimension_match`, not by JSON Schema.

time.description: Duration or an instant on a declared clock.

frequency.description: Events or cycles per unit time.

dimensionless.description: Pure number: counts, probabilities, ratios, coefficients.

per_time.description: Reciprocal time. Produced by density normalization over a time-binned axis.

per_voltage.description: Reciprocal voltage. Produced by density normalization over a voltage-binned axis.

per_dimensionless.description: Reciprocal of a pure number. Produced by density normalization over a dimensionless axis such as degree.

simulator_defined.description: A quantity whose unit is defined by a source simulator and has no safe SI mapping. Never silently converted, never compared across systems.

units.label: weight (NEST)
units.note: NEST synaptic weight. Its physical meaning depends on the synapse and neuron model (it may act as a current, a conductance, or a dimensionless factor). Cortexel never converts it, and never pools it across models.

units.label: ms
units.note: NEST delays are expressed in milliseconds and are a genuine time; unlike weight they map safely to SI.

quantityKinds.description: An instant on a declared clock, or a lag.
A length of time: a bin width, a window, an interval.
The interval between two successive events of the SAME train. Never formed across senders or trials.
Synaptic transmission delay. Must be finite and positive.
Cycles or events per unit time.
Event rate. The denominator (per neuron, per trial, total) is a separate recorded fact, not part of the unit.
Membrane potential. Only use this kind when the source declares the signal IS a membrane potential.
Any other electric potential.
Electric current.
Electric conductance.
Concentration of a declared chemical species (for example calcium or IP3).
A spatial coordinate in a declared coordinate frame.
A spatial extent or distance.
A plane angle or phase.
An exact non-negative integer count of observed things.
A node degree: an exact non-negative integer.
A value in [0,1] whose group sums to 1.
A density over a binned axis: integrating value x binWidth over the bins yields 1. Its unit is the reciprocal of the binned axis unit.
A correlation coefficient. Only use this kind when a recognized formula and its required statistics are present; a scaled pair count is NOT a correlation coefficient.
A dimensionless ratio or normalized value.
A synaptic weight. Its dimension depends on the synapse model; declare it. Weights of different declared dimensions are never pooled.
A model state variable in a phase plane or multi-signal trace, whose physical dimension is declared.
A time derivative of a state variable, used by the phase-plane vector field.

conversionPolicy.sameDimensionOnly: A conversion is legal only between two codes of the same dimension. Cross-dimension conversion is never attempted.
conversionPolicy.simulatorDefinedNeverConverted: A code whose dimension is `simulator_defined` has `toCanonical: null` and can never be converted, compared, or pooled with any other code — including another simulator-defined code.
conversionPolicy.aliasesAreNotCanonical: Aliases are accepted ONLY by adapters and by `cortexel migrate`. A stable 1.0 request that uses an alias is rejected with a repair suggesting the canonical code. Silent alias conversion in the normal validation path is prohibited.
conversionPolicy.conversionsAreRecorded: Every conversion performed during canonicalization appends a derivation operation recording `from`, `to`, a rounded display factor, the exact rational numerator/denominator plus binary exponent, and the one-rounding algorithm id. The display factor alone is not a replay authority.
conversionPolicy.exactFactors: A registered power-of-ten scale is interpreted as that exact decimal rational, not as the quotient of two rounded binary64 constants. Other scales use the exact registered binary64 value. Conversion forms the exact scale ratio and rounds once to binary64; it never chains through an intermediate unit or double-rounds a factor.
